title: Top Tech News Today, July 20, 2026: Alibaba, Bezos, Blackstone, Google, Moonshot AI, Nvidia, Samsung & More
snippet: The European Commission adopted binding requirements under the Digital Markets Act ordering Google to open Android to rival AI assistants and to share portions of its search data with competitors. Search data sharing begins in January 2027, with Android interoperability due by July 2027. Also covered: Blackstone's roughly $676 million investment in Futronic, a South Korean maker of precision actuators used in robotics and automotive systems.

title: AI News Today July 20 2026: 16 Biggest Stories
snippet: A roundup of the day's biggest AI industry stories including model releases, funding rounds, and semiconductor developments.

title: Microsoft Just Put $2.5 Billion Into Helping You Use AI (And Other AI Business News)
snippet: Microsoft launched Frontier Co., a $2.5 billion unit staffed with around 6,000 engineers, consultants and specialists who go into client companies and work next to their teams to get AI running inside daily work, addressing the gap between AI adoption and successful implementation.

title: AI News. July 20, 2026
snippet: Daily roundup of AI application and enterprise adoption news.

title: Blackstone invests roughly $676 million in Futronic, South Korean precision actuator maker
snippet: Blackstone invested in Futronic, a South Korean manufacturer of high-precision actuators used in automotive systems and industrial robots, valuing the company at approximately 1 trillion won (about $676 million). Actuators are essential components in robotic arms, automated production systems, and emerging humanoid robots.

title: China Sends Robots Out Into the World to Learn How to Be Human
snippet: In an industrial park outside Beijing, humanoid robots are being deployed as workers film themselves performing tasks like grabbing cushions and folding sheets to help develop robot 'brains' through imitation learning.

title: Kelun scores sac-TMT win in first-line NSCLC population missing from Merck's massive phase 3 program
snippet: Kelun-Biotech's Phase III OptiTROP-Lung06 trial of sacituzumab tirumotecan (sac-TMT) plus Merck's Keytruda met its primary endpoint of progression-free survival in first-line PD-L1-negative non-squamous NSCLC. The sponsor calls it the first Phase 3 result for an antibody-drug conjugate combined with a checkpoint inhibitor to replace platinum-based chemotherapy in this first-line setting.

title: Other news to note for July 20, 2026
snippet: Roundup of smaller biotech and pharma industry news items for the day.

title: YouTube clarifies that creators can't monetize "generic or repetitive content," content that's "unsatisfying or off-putting," or content with fake AI "experts"
snippet: YouTube updated its Inauthentic Content Policy language to clarify three categories that make a channel ineligible for monetization: generic or repetitive templated content, content that is unsatisfying or off-putting to viewers, and content featuring fake AI 'experts.' The underlying rules have not changed; the update just gives creators clearer, more actionable language for why a channel gets flagged.

title: YouTube refreshes Studio and updates video guidance
snippet: YouTube announced updates to YouTube Studio and refreshed guidance for video creators covering upload workflows and content policy references.
